growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron is turning AI memory scarcity into contracted demand: on July 9 it announced up to $3B of U.S. supply-chain investment and a 10-year GlobalWafers deal, while its June 24 earnings remarks said data-center revenue was running above a $25B annualized pace and tight conditions should persist beyond 2027.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI memory scarcity, long-term customer agreements, and U.S. supply-chain investment outweigh cyclicality at a low forward multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Ross Bhappu bought 74,000 shares for about $968K on July 7, and the company is simultaneously pushing a $1.9B VAC acquisition plus July rare-earth circuit modifications, giving the insider buy real operational backing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO open-market buying plus the VAC mine-to-magnet deal create a rare actionable catalyst in a beaten-down uranium and rare-earth name.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The stock has sold off sharply after a fiscal Q3 revenue-growth cut and is now roughly 40% below its 2026 highs, but Morningstar still treats Accenture as a wide-moat business with durable AI-booking momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Accenture looks like an oversold quality compounder where AI-booking durability and cash flow are being priced too cheaply.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow is packaging AI into monetizable SKUs rather than just demos: its July 9 Now Assist release notes consolidated AI and asset-management capabilities under an AI-native SKU strategy, and the company already raised FY2026 subscription guidance on the back of AI demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Overlap signal plus 50% drawdown, strong FCF, AI SKU monetization, and imminent Q2 earnings create an attractive enterprise software reset.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Citi beat Q2 earnings but sold off anyway because management held return guidance steady, which is exactly the kind of fresh post-earnings dislocation that can create a large-cap value entry point.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Citi's Q2 beat, best quarterly revenue in a decade, improving RoTCE, and buyback/dividend capacity support a large-bank value buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Atlassian is still an AI-disruption candidate because the market is pricing in pressure on seat-based SaaS economics: on July 14 it scrapped equity refresh grants amid a share-price slump tied to AI fears, even as the company leans harder into Rovo and agentic workflows.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Overlap signal, 56% drawdown, 30%+ revenue growth, strong FCF, and Rovo AI adoption outweigh SaaS disruption fears at a low forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv opened a new Johor, Malaysia facility on July 1 to expand power, cooling, and integrated infrastructure capacity for AI and high-density computing across Asia, which directly addresses the bottleneck that hyperscale AI data centers are facing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv has direct exposure to the AI data-center power and cooling bottleneck, with capacity expansion and earnings catalyst support.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal is still a large profitable franchise, but it is roughly 40% below its 52-week high and only about 23% above the low; Q2 earnings are due on July 28, 2026, which gives the stock a near-term reset point if margins and buybacks hold up, though competition and TPV deceleration remain the main bear cases.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is a cheap, cash-generative reset candidate with Q2 earnings close enough to force a margin and buyback verdict.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Zoetis is more than 50% below its 52-week high after a rough run of pet-health sentiment, yet it remains a wide-moat animal-health leader with a resilient recurring-demand profile.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Zoetis is a high-margin animal-health leader trading like a broken pet-stock cycle despite durable category demand.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Trump's NATO-summit pledge to license Ukraine to produce Patriot systems, plus Reuters-reported talks on a European Patriot maintenance facility, extends Lockheed's relevance beyond direct system sales into long-duration sustainment and allied production support.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed offers a better defense entry than RTX because the Patriot catalyst meets a cheaper valuation and deeper drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's tightening rare-earth export controls and retaliatory export restrictions on U.S. firms explicitly hit MP Materials, reinforcing the scarcity premium on non-China supply and increasing the strategic value of domestic rare-earth processing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is a speculative strategic-minerals buy where non-China rare-earth scarcity and short interest offset weak current profitability.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The renewed Iran-Hormuz escalation pushed crude sharply higher and set U.S. oil majors up for record Q2 profits, which directly benefits Exxon as a low-cost, globally diversified producer with immediate leverage to a geopolitically driven oil risk premium.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Exxon has a near-term earnings windfall from higher oil prices while still trading at a reasonable forward multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anastasios Arima bought 138,720 shares for about $497K on Jul 10 while Executive Chairman Todd Hannigan bought 225,000 shares for about $805K on Jul 10 and another 300,000 shares for about $1.08M on Jul 13, creating a $2.38M leadership cluster buy after weakness in a company with meaningful operating leverage to titanium commercialization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IperionX is a speculative insider-cluster and U.S. titanium supply-chain play with meaningful upside but real financing risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Director Silvestre Vila Moret bought five times between Jun 26 and Jul 8, adding 792,615 shares for about $4.2M in total, which is a sustained open-market accumulation pattern in a large-cap Argentine bank rather than a one-off signal.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Grupo Galicia is a high-risk Argentina bank recovery trade backed by repeated director buying and cheap forward earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Multiple insiders, including the CEO, have been buying Liberty Latin America while the stock trades around a $1.5B market cap and analysts still see double-digit upside, which is a cleaner cluster-buy setup than most telecom names.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider buying by the CEO and major holders, positive FCF, and 50%+ analyst upside make LILA a credible small-cap telecom value setup despite leverage.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Viatris has a defined binary catalyst with the MR-141 presbyopia program, which carries a confirmed October 17, 2026 PDUFA after positive Phase 3 data, creating a real late-year rerating setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Low forward multiple, strong FCF, and multiple FDA decision dates give Viatris a catalyst-backed value case.

catalysts_general: Discovery: President and CEO Peter R. Matt bought 8,230 shares on Jul 10 for about $504K, a straightforward CEO open-market purchase in a profitable industrial with a multi-billion-dollar market cap, which usually carries more weight than smaller director nibblers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO open-market buying, cheap forward valuation, and recent earnings strength make CMC a solid cyclical industrial value pick.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Goldman Sachs upgraded NIO to Buy yesterday with a $7 target, and the ADR still trades far below its 52-week high, so the name has a fresh analyst catalyst alongside a sizable turnaround gap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Goldman's fresh upgrade, strong Q2 deliveries, and 40%+ target upside make NIO a high-risk EV turnaround buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista remains one of the cleanest AI networking beneficiaries: Q1 2026 revenue grew 35.1% to $2.709B, the company kept pushing its 1.6T Ethernet roadmap, and recent market action shows investors still treating it as a core hyperscaler AI-fabric winner.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is a high-quality AI Ethernet winner, but the stock is near highs and priced for near-perfect execution.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Applied Materials has a direct AI wafer-fab catalyst: it introduced new DRAM and advanced-packaging systems on June 25, and Meta's July 9 Iris chip plan pushed WFE names higher, reinforcing AMAT as a levered beneficiary of leading-edge and memory capex.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Applied Materials is well placed for AI wafer-fab and advanced-packaging demand, but current valuation already discounts a strong WFE cycle.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Palantir continues to show real enterprise AI monetization: its Q1 2026 update showed U.S. commercial revenue up 133% and TCV up 45%, and its July release cycle added pro-code Foundry agents, MCP support, and new customer expansion in Mexico and insurance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Palantir has real AI monetization and exceptional margins, but valuation and political-contract scrutiny cap risk/reward.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Nike has been sold down hard, sitting about 46.5% below its 52-week high and just 7.1% above the low; the next earnings date is September 29, 2026, so this is a classic capitulation setup if management can show inventory and demand stabilization, but a slow recovery in wholesale and China would keep the bear case alive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nike is washed out enough to be tempting, but China weakness and uneven margin recovery keep it from being a clean buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Seagate is one of the clearest AI-storage beneficiaries right now: Wells Fargo raised its target and the stock still trades well below its June high ahead of late-July earnings.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Seagate is a real AI-storage beneficiary, but the stock has already priced in a large part of the HDD shortage thesis.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Hims & Hers still has nearly 30% of its float sold short, but revenue keeps growing and the Novo Nordisk partnership gives the stock a real commercial catalyst that shorts are still leaning against.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Hims has squeeze potential and a GLP-1 platform catalyst, but regulatory and valuation risks are too unresolved for a buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The NATO summit in Ankara moved Raytheon's AMRAAM program into European co-production for the first time, which should support backlog growth and de-risk missile supply at a moment when allies are racing to replenish air-defense inventories.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has strong defense-demand visibility, but valuation and proximity to highs make it more of a hold than a new-money buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Higher crude and diesel prices from the Middle East shock widen refining margins and support a stronger earnings setup for Valero, which was singled out alongside Marathon as a beneficiary of the current fuel-price spike.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Valero benefits from refining-margin strength, but the stock is at its 52-week high and above consensus target.

catalysts_general: Discovery: ICONIQ-linked 10% owner William J.G. Griffith accumulated 610,291 shares on Jul 8 at about $11.82, then added 241,628 shares on Jul 10 and 64,771 shares on Jul 13 for roughly $11.0M total, a fund-led stake build in a $5.72B market cap software name that looks like real institutional conviction rather than a token insider trade.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Netskope has credible insider accumulation and cybersecurity growth, but losses and valuation keep it below buy status.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Dollar Tree reauthorized its repurchase program to an aggregate $2.5B on Jul 2 and noted it had already repurchased $500M in June 2026 via a block trade tied to Mantle Ridge-affiliated sellers, a large capital-return signal from a retailer that is actively buying stock while valuation is compressed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Large July buyback authorization supports downside, but valuation is only fair and analyst upside is essentially exhausted.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Stellantis is 53.4% below its 52-week high and only 8.4% above the low, yet the stock still has a concrete catalyst with Q2 earnings on July 30, 2026 and a very low forward multiple; the market is pricing a harsh reset, but tariff pressure, Europe weakness, and EV execution are the obvious risks.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Stellantis is statistically cheap after a 50%+ drawdown, but negative FCF and tariff/EV execution risk keep it from being a clean turnaround buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Kyndryl has been crushed to a deep discount after a long slide, but recent coverage argues the market is still underestimating the turnaround in profitability and cash generation after the IBM spinoff.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Kyndryl is deeply discounted with cash-flow optionality, but revenue decline, guidance cuts, and high leverage make the turnaround unproven.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Nuvalent's zidesamtinib NDA has a September 18, 2026 PDUFA date, and the company says the FDA review is anchored by ARROS-1 data, making this a clean high-volatility biotech catalyst.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nuvalent has a clean September PDUFA catalyst, but the stock is already at its high with no revenue and negative FCF.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Benzinga flagged a bullish call sweep in PNC on Jul 13 with 196 contracts at the Jul 24, 2026 $250 strike for about $119.5K across 39 split trades, which is the kind of near-dated, high-conviction flow traders watch ahead of a catalyst window.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PNC is a high-quality overlap bank, but it is near its 52-week high with limited analyst upside, so the risk/reward is not compelling for new money.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Harley-Davidson has a 16.39% short float and a 5.88 days-to-cover ratio on Finviz, with 17.07M shares sold short and second-quarter results due Jul 23, so this is a plausible squeeze candidate if the upcoming print improves sentiment even modestly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Harley has squeeze potential into earnings, but negative revenue/earnings trends and low target upside make it too weak for a fundamental buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Bloom is a second-order AI infrastructure play on power scarcity: Brookfield and Bloom expanded their AI infrastructure partnership to $25B on June 30, and recent market coverage says on-site power is gaining share as grid access and community opposition slow new AI data centers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The Brookfield AI-power deal is real, but the stock already prices in perfection with extreme multiples, high leverage, and short-seller controversy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IBM's July 14 preliminary Q2 warning is a warning shot for legacy software exposure: customers shifted spend toward servers, storage, and memory ahead of AI-related price hikes, and IBM's software and infrastructure revenue both came in weaker than expected.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IBM's 35% drawdown is not enough after a fresh Q2 warning exposed software/infrastructure weakness and AI-driven budget shifts.
